hypothesis: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (W/F/Y) will enhance SSTR2-specific TM5 complementarity, while mutating position 11 to hydrophobic residues (L/I/V) will improve ECL2 contact specificity and reduce off-target affinity.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The campaign is making progress in stability and structural design but is still limited by off-target interactions. The next iteration should prioritize mutations at non-pharmacophore positions to improve selectivity.

proposed_changes.rationale: These positions are non-pharmacophore and are known to contribute to off-target interactions (especially with SSTR3/SSTR5). Focusing mutations here can disrupt conserved-pocket contacts while preserving the pharmacophore (positions 7-10 and Cys3/Cys14).

These mutations aim to reduce hydrophobic or conserved interactions at key off-target interface positions without compromising the pharmacophore. For example, introducing polar residues at position 1 or 2 can reduce SSTR3/SSTR5 binding.
